# ARC-QUIC-CRT-0011 - QUIC Server-Role EncryptedExtensions Continuation Architecture

## Purpose

Describe the permanent server-role local EncryptedExtensions continuation that lives behind the existing transcript-progress and bridge-driver seam, extends the server floor after local ServerHello and Handshake key availability, formats local transport parameters into one local EncryptedExtensions message, and exposes outbound EncryptedExtensions bytes through the existing library-owned handshake seam without claiming certificate flight, server Finished, or interop support.

## Requirements Satisfied

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0113

## Design Summary

The connection library keeps server-role local EncryptedExtensions emission as the next bridge-owned slice after the existing server floor rather than adding a second TLS stack. The existing transcript-progress owner remains responsible for ordered Handshake CRYPTO assembly, deterministic message framing checks, and peer transport-parameter staging from ClientHello. Once that owner has accepted a supported ClientHello for TLS 1.3 with TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 over secp256r1 and the managed key-schedule owner has already published the local ServerHello and Handshake key availability, the key schedule formats one deterministic local EncryptedExtensions message from the local QUIC transport parameters, appends that message behind the local ServerHello at the transcript boundary, and exposes the local EncryptedExtensions bytes as Handshake-level CryptoDataAvailable with the correct offset semantics on the existing handshake seam. The slice deliberately stops after local EncryptedExtensions and does not synthesize certificate messages, server Finished, commit, endpoint-host wiring, or interop-runner handshake support.

## Key Components

- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransport.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTransportTlsBridgeState.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransportBridgeDriver.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTranscriptProgress.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsKeySchedule.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsCipherSuiteProfile.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0112.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0113.cs

## Risks

- The server-role EE continuation must stay behind the existing bridge seam and must not grow into a broad TLS subsystem.
- The supported subset must stay pinned to TLS 1.3 with TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 over secp256r1 until a later requirement explicitly widens it.
- Outbound EncryptedExtensions availability must not be mistaken for certificate-flight ownership, handshake completion, or interop support.
- Server-role commit must remain blocked even though Handshake keys become available at the ClientHello plus ServerHello transcript boundary and local EncryptedExtensions is emitted on the existing handshake seam.
